## Status and development of the German data centre landscape – Executive Summary

#### bmwk.de

Imprint

Publisher 
Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action (BMWK) 
Public Relations 
10100 Berlin 
www.bmwk.de 

Status March 2025

This brochure is available for download only.

Design PRpetuum GmbH, 81541 Munich

This publication is issued by the Federal Ministry for Economic Affairs and Climate Action as part of its public relations work. The publication is available free of charge. It is not for sale and may not be used by political parties or groups for electoral campaigning.

### Executive Summary

The growth of the digital economy and digitalisa tion is accompanied by a strong increase in the need for digital infrastructure and data centres (DCs). Data centres have thus developed into one of the fastest growing, most energy-intensive sectors in Germany. With over 2,000 data centres and an installed IT power demand of over 2,700 MW, Ger many is already the largest centre for digital infra structure in Europe. The central location, political stability, an electricity supply with above-average reliability and one of the largest internet connec tions in the world combine to make Germany a strategically advantageous place for national and international providers alike. Digitalisation targets, a growing digital economy and new technologies, such as artificial intelligence (AI), lead to growing demand for processing power, storage capacity and data transfer. This

## DATA CENTER IMPACT REPORT DEUTSCHLAND

#### WWW.GERMANDATACENTERS.COM

1,994 data centers*

### THE DATA CENTER MARKET IN FIGURES

thereof 309 colocation data centers thereof 69 % in colocation data centers

1,955 MW IT power in Germany approx.

65.000 Employees through data centers

88 % Energy use from RE in colocation data centers 10.4 billion € German colocation and hyperscale data centers contribute to GDP annually 28 % of the colocation data centers utilise the waste heat

831 MW IT power in Frankfurt colocation data centers * Colocation and enterprise data centers > 50 kW

DEAR READERS,

Hardly any situation in our modern everyday lives is con ceivable without digital data – whether we are exchang ing messages on our smartphone, checking public trans port connections in an app or following the day‘s news online on the way to work. But the control of large parts of critical infrastructure – traffic management systems, power plants, heat and electricity grids – also rely on un interrupted data streams.

This data is stored, processed, and transmitted in data centers. In recent decades, these data centers have mostly been located in secret, away from social debate, where they ensured the reliable provision of the services that we all use – directly or indirectly.

Data centers have recently become the focus of public attention due to the German Energy Efficiency Act, which places explicit efficiency requirements on existing and new facilities. Regulatory initiatives su

results.title: New 2026 State of European Data Centres | eudca.org
results.text:
New 2026 State of European Data Centres | eudca.org

Skip to Content

## New State of European Data Centres 2026 report

## State of European Data Centres 2026

The State of European Data Centres 2026 is EUDCA’s flagship annual report, delivering a data-driven overview of Europe’s data centre sector. Developed with national trade associations (NTAs) and leading researchers, the report examines the infrastructure supporting Europe’s digital economy and the forces that will shape its future.

The 2026 edition shows that commercial colocation and hyperscale facilities now provide more than two-thirds of Europe’s IT power, driven by the growing demand for cloud and AI. It explores shifting regional growth patterns across Southern Europe and the Nordics, alongside the impact of new connectivity, renewable energy investment and rising power constraints.

With European IT power demand forecast to grow at a CAGR of 17% through 2031, the report analyses the key challenges, including access to power, cooling innovation, and its growing socio-economic and sustainability contribution. Colocation data centres significantly advanced Europe's 2030 energy, water, and climate goals in 2025 alone, contributing €53 billion to the continent's GDP.

Whether you’re a policymaker, investor, data centre operator, or digital strategist, this report is the critical intelligence you need to navigate the future of digital infrastructure in Europe.

##### Enter your details for instant access

results.title: State of European Data Centres 2025 | eudca.org
results.text:
State of European Data Centres 2025 | eudca.org

Skip to Content

# Inaugural Flagship Report - State of European Data Centres 2025 Click here to download​​

## State of European Data Centres 2025

The State of European Data Centres 2025 is the inaugural flagship report from the European Data Centre Association (EUDCA), offering an unprecedented deep dive into the infrastructure that powers Europe’s digital economy. Developed in collaboration with National Trade Associations and leading researchers, this full report goes far beyond the management summary - presenting a large, data-driven examination of the economic, environmental, and technological landscape of Europe’s colocation and hyperscale data centres.

Inside, the report reveals how €100 billion in investments are reshaping Europe’s data centre landscape, advancing AI, boosting digital sovereignty, and expanding from FLAP-D hubs to fast-growing Nordic and Southern regions. It highlights a projected €83.8 billion GDP impact and over 80,000 jobs by 2030, while addressing key challenges like power access, skills shortages, and sustainability, underscored by the sector’s use of 94% renewable energy.

Whether you’re a policymaker, investor, data centre operator, or digital strategist, this report is the critical intelligence you need to navigate the future of digital infrastructure in Europe.

results.title: European Data Center Overview | eudca.org
results.text:
European Data Center Overview | eudca.org

Skip to Content

## European Data Centre Overview

The European Data Center Overview highlights the diversity of the European data center ecosystem, taking into account the added value for our European societies. Additionally, it showcases how data centers operate across Europe. Moreover, the report explores the types of energy data centers use and the sustainability initiatives data centers are implementing.

As a first of its kind, this report aims to create a comprehensive overview of the current European data center market based on the available facts, figures, and examples. Resulting in a knowledge base on the social, environmental, and economic value of this European sector. The European Data Center Overview was created through the fruitful collaboration of several European data center associations. We hope you enjoy reading the report and find it useful for the understanding of the European data center industry.

---

The Switzerland data center colocation market is expected to grow by 15.0% on an annual basis to reach US$779.0 million in 2026. The colocation market in Switzerland has demonstrated consistent expansion during 2021-2025, recording a CAGR of 14.1%.

This growth momentum is accelerate over the forecast period, with the market projected to register a 10.9% from 2026-2030. By the end of 2030, the colocation market is anticipated to expand from US$677.4 million in 2025 to approximately US$1.17 billion, driven by surging AI and GPU workload demand, accelerating hyperscaler capacity build-out, and sustained enterprise adoption of hybrid multi-cloud infrastructure.This report provides a structured, data-centric analysis of the US data center colocation market, offering comprehensive coverage of both the overall data center landscape and the colocation ecosystem. results.text:
Switzerland Data Center Colocation Market - Supply & Demand Analysis 2025-2030

€2230EUR$2,500USD£1,928GBP

View Pricing

1h Free Analyst Time

Speak directly to the analyst to clarify any post sales queries you may have.

The Switzerland Data Center Colocation Market was valued at USD 465 million in 2024, and is projected to reach USD 916 million by 2030, rising at a CAGR of 11.96%.

The Swiss data center colocation market is one of the fastest-growing colocation markets in Western Europe. It is a landlocked country; it does not have any submarine cables. However, it is well connected to the global internet infrastructure via underground fibre-optic cables that link Switzerland with neighbouring countries, including Germany, France, Italy, Austria, and others.In Switzerland, the demand for artificial intelligence (AI) is continuously increasing due to the growing use of AI applications across various industries, including healthcare and transportation, among others. We anticipate that this demand will continue to rise in the coming years. Additionally, we have observed that some data center operators in Switzerland have already begun investing in AI-ready data centers.Some of the leading colocation operators in the market include Digital Realty, Equinix, Green Datacenter, NTT DATA, NorthC, STACK Infrastructure, Vantage Data Centers and several others.The average occupancy rate of colocation data centers across the country was over 90% which is likely to rise to
